metadata.title: Find a Study Spot on Campus
metadata.disclaimer: This dataset is an informational guide for finding study, quiet, calm, and reflection spaces. Hours, access rules, room availability, and bookings can change. Confirm live details with the University of Alberta Library, campus maps, LibCal, or the owning unit before publishing.
metadata.sourcePolicy: Official University of Alberta Library and University of Alberta pages were prioritized. Where direct UAlberta pages were blocked by JavaScript verification, the attached Campus Maps.docx was treated as a seed source and those entries are marked for manual browser review.
retrievalNotes: The attached Campus Maps.docx was used to extract campus map, calm space, study area, and prayer/reflection links.
The University of Alberta Library study area directory was fully retrievable and is the primary source for library noise zones.

Several www.ualberta.ca pages returned JavaScript verification or 403 pages in this environment; those entries are retained only when they appeared in the provided document and are marked needsReview.

hero.title: Find a study spot on campus
hero.subtitle: Quiet zones, silent spaces, discussion areas, bookable rooms, and calm places to reset.

quickRecommendations.title: Quiet or silent study
quickRecommendations.summary: Best options for focused solo study with limited or no talking.

quickRecommendations.title: Discussion and group work
quickRecommendations.summary: Spaces where normal conversation or group work is allowed.

quickRecommendations.title: Late-night or 24/7 study
quickRecommendations.summary: Spaces with official 24/7 or after-hours study access.

quickRecommendations.title: Calm, prayer, and reflection
quickRecommendations.summary: Not general study rooms, but useful for a quiet reset between classes.

noiseZoneGuide.label: Silent Study Zone
noiseZoneGuide.plainLanguage: No talking. Phones should be silent.

noiseZoneGuide.label: Quiet Study Zone
noiseZoneGuide.plainLanguage: Whisper only. Phones should be on vibrate.

noiseZoneGuide.label: Discussion Zone
noiseZoneGuide.plainLanguage: Normal conversation and group work are allowed.

spaces.name: Cameron Library
spaces.address: 11231 Saskatchewan Drive, University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.roomOrArea: Main floor, basement, 2nd floor common area, and 3rd floor

areas: Knowledge Common and Study Hall, Basement

spaces.summary: Science and engineering library with discussion areas, quiet basement study, a silent 3rd floor, bookable rooms, extensive computing, and 24/7 access to the basement Knowledge Common and Study Hall for staff and students.
amenities: 18 bookable study rooms on the main floor and basement
Over 400 Windows computing workstations listed on the Cameron location page
Bookable group study and presentation preparation rooms with computers and LCD displays

access.accessNotes: General areas are drop-in during library hours. The basement Knowledge Common and Study Hall are listed as 24/7 for staff and students only with ONEcard required for entry.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Cameron discussion, quiet, and silent zone locations and general study amenities.
Verifies address, 18 bookable rooms, 24/7 basement study access, and over 400 Windows computing workstations.

spaces.name: Cameron Library Listening Space
spaces.address: 11231 Saskatchewan Drive, University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.summary: A therapeutic space on the first floor of Cameron Library using natural and hybrid soundscapes intended to support mindful calm and wellbeing while students relax, study, or socialize.

access.accessNotes: Use during Cameron Library access hours unless local signage says otherwise.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Cameron 1-40, first-floor location, intended use, and wellbeing purpose.

spaces.name: Rutherford Library North
spaces.address: University of Alberta, 11208 89 Avenue, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.summary: Large humanities, social sciences, and education library with over 700 study spaces across five floors, plus discussion, quiet, and silent areas.

access.accessNotes: General study spaces are drop-in during library hours. Study rooms are bookable.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Rutherford North discussion, quiet, silent zones and studying/computing counts.
Verifies address, library profile, computer workstation availability, and bookable study rooms.

spaces.name: Rutherford Library South
spaces.address: University of Alberta, 11208 89 Avenue, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.summary: Rutherford South provides a quieter reading room and discussion foyer, plus nearly 500 study spaces and IST computer labs.

access.accessNotes: Use during Rutherford Library hours.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Rutherford South discussion and quiet zones, study space count, and computer labs.

spaces.name: Rutherford Library Family Corner
spaces.address: University of Alberta, 11208 89 Avenue, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.summary: A registered-access, child-friendly space for students and employees who parent or care for children, with study stations, computers, a conference table, lactation space, stroller parking, and child-friendly supplies.
amenities: Study stations with computers and study equipment
Small kitchen area with sink, kettle, bottle warmers, and equipment

access.accessNotes: Registration with Student Success and Experience is required. Approved users access the room with ONEcard and must supervise children at all times.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Rutherford Room 3-114, registration and ONEcard access, and family study amenities.

spaces.name: Geoffrey and Robyn Sperber Health Sciences Library
spaces.building: Dianne and Irving Kipnes Health Research Academy
spaces.address: 1-120 Dianne and Irving Kipnes Health Research Academy, 11405 87 Avenue NW, Edmonton, AB T6G 1C9

areas: Level 1 east side outside the Teaching and Learning and Maker Labs
Teaching and Learning Lab if available
Entrance area by service desk and stairs
Level 1 west side by the Indigenous Learning Space and Indigenous Healing Collection
Lower Level around the stairs leading to silent study space
Lower Level back half starting at the RC 424.7 book stacks and study room L1-152A

spaces.summary: Health sciences library with clearly separated discussion, quiet, and silent zones plus 22 bookable study rooms on the main and lower levels.
amenities: 22 bookable study rooms on the main and lower levels
12 group study rooms and 10 individual study rooms listed on the booking page
Group study rooms with monitors for laptop connection
Windows computing workstations on the main floor

access.accessNotes: General areas are drop-in during library hours. Group and individual rooms can be booked.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Sperber discussion, quiet, silent zones and study room features.
Verifies address, 22 bookable rooms, Indigenous Learning Space, and computing.

spaces.name: Augustana Library
spaces.summary: Augustana Library has discussion study on the main floor, quiet study on the lower level, bookable study rooms and booths, computer workstations, and a 24/7 study space with after-hours entrance access.
amenities: Tables, chairs, and soft seating near power outlets
Treadmill desk listed on the Augustana location page

access.accessNotes: The 24/7 study space is accessible through the south Forum entrance after hours, but is closed for statutory holidays and during summer months.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Augustana discussion and quiet zones plus general amenities.
Verifies address, bookable rooms and booths, 24/7 study access notes, and computing.

spaces.name: Bibliotheque Saint-Jean
spaces.address: 8406 rue Marie-Anne Gaboury, Edmonton, AB T6C 4G9
spaces.roomOrArea: Main floor service areas, study carrels, tables, and group study rooms

areas: Main floor service areas near the welcome desk and printer
Main floor study carrels and tables in the general and education collections

spaces.summary: Campus Saint-Jean library with quiet carrels and tables, discussion areas near service points, five bookable group study rooms, and one silent study room.
amenities: 48 study carrels, including 2 with adjustable desks
5 group study rooms for 2 to 6 people
Rooms 1-12F and 1-12H have wall-mounted LCD screens
6 Windows computers listed in the study area directory
Advanced booking required for group rooms

access.accessNotes: Check current hours before visiting. The library hours page listed a spring/summer closure notice when checked.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies BSJ discussion and quiet zones, carrels, group rooms, screens, and silent study room.
Verifies address and 5 bookable study rooms.

reviewNotes: Verify current semester hours before publishing because the library hours page displayed a closure note for spring/summer.

spaces.name: Library Bookable Study Rooms
spaces.roomOrArea: Bookable rooms and booths across participating libraries
spaces.summary: Central booking option for study rooms across Cameron, Augustana, Bibliotheque Saint-Jean, Rutherford, Sperber, and Weir Library.
amenities: Augustana Library: 7 study rooms and 3 study booths
Rutherford Library: 7 rooms on the booking page; Rutherford location page separately says 5 rooms on the second, fourth, and fifth levels
Sperber Library: 12 group study rooms and 10 individual study rooms
Weir Library: booking/policies routed to Faculty of Law information

access.accessNotes: Study rooms are available for booking during library hours by U of A students and staff only. Rooms may be booked up to seven days in advance. Regular term bookings are up to 2 hours per day; exam period bookings are up to 4 hours per day.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies participating libraries, counts, booking rules, booking window, daily limits, and room-use policies.

reviewNotes: There is a count discrepancy for Rutherford: the central booking page lists 7 rooms while the Rutherford location page says 5 bookable rooms. Confirm in LibCal before publishing room counts prominently.

spaces.name: Digital Scholarship Centre
spaces.address: Cameron Library, 11231 Saskatchewan Dr NW #2-20, Edmonton, AB T6G 2J8
spaces.summary: Research and training facility with makerspace, studio space, high-performance computers, digital technology, workshops, and consultations.
amenities: Studio spaces for digitization, gaming, virtual reality, audio, photography, or videography
Bookable consultations and certification practice sessions

access.accessNotes: Many DSC tools and spaces require certification, consultation, or booking. Check DSC pages before visiting for project work.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies location and creative spaces including makerspaces, studio space, and high-performance computers.
Verifies DSC purpose, consultations, workshops, technology, and making.

spaces.name: Weir Law Library
spaces.summary: Law Library study space managed by the Faculty of Law. The library hours page says the space is open to all U of A students and staff during Law Centre hours with ONEcard access.
amenities: 15 rooms listed on one version of the library booking page
Study room policies routed to Faculty of Law information on another version of the booking page

access.accessNotes: Tap ONEcard for access during Law Centre hours. Confirm Faculty of Law room booking policies before relying on bookable room availability.

sourceEvidence.claim: Verifies Weir Library access is via ONEcard and open to all U of A students and staff during Law Centre hours.
One retrieved version listed 15 Weir Library study rooms; another version routes users to the Weir webpage for policy details.

reviewNotes: The direct Weir Library UAlberta page returned a JavaScript verification page in this environment.
Confirm current room counts and booking rules in a normal browser before publishing detailed Weir room information.
